note: Golden judge response (#54) for the multi-artifact case: exercises full-relative-path attribution (specs/001-orders/plan.md), unique-basename fallback (tasks.md), the unattributed bucket (an artifact name that resolves to nothing must be kept visible under scoring's synthetic '(unattributed)' review, never dropped), a null pitfall_id, and synonym normalization ('warning' -> medium, 'consistent' -> consistency).

findings.message: Technical Context states choices (server-side PDF generation, per-order cache) with no rationale tied to a requirement.
findings.suggestion: Add a one-line rationale per choice linking it to the requirement it serves.

findings.message: The spec promises invoices for every completed order but the plan caches PDFs per order with an undecided retention period — the artifacts disagree on invoice availability.
findings.suggestion: Decide retention in the plan and reconcile it with the spec's 100% availability criterion.

findings.message: Phase 1 is titled 'User Story 1' but the Dependencies section speaks generically of 'a story'; task phases and dependencies drift apart as stories are added.
findings.suggestion: Name the dependency edges per story (US1 -> US2) instead of generically.
